Scope

The Scope of this document included the Phase-I Qlik Sense implementation of the Medical report. The dashboard shows the lifecycle of an interaction. How the medical service liaison reaches out to a HCP/HCA, how the information is shared in terms of an Interaction or a Presentation, the details being discussed, the mindset of the HCP/HCAs and getting the information and tracking the journey of the various inquiries (MIRs) sent by the HCP/HCAs for a grievance.
Data Sources: Veeva, CIM, Field Deployment
section_path: Data Sources: Veeva, CIM, Field Deployment

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Business Rules

Columns: Rule, Rule/Description
Row 1: Time, Starting from July 2017 to the latest week’s data
Row 2: Time, Both Medical HCA and HCP reports are refreshed on a weekly basis
Row 3: User Security, Section Access based on geographical assignment of medical team
Row 4: Sales Teams - HCP, 
Row 5: Sales Teams - HCA, 
Row 6: Business Units, Diabetes, CV, Biosurgery, Oncology, Endo, MS, Rare Disease, Genetic Disease etc.
Row 7: Customer Universe, All Affiliated Physicians and Accounts – Current Profiles
Row 8: Calls, No Call Only/Sample Only calls
Call Status should be in Submitted Only
Row 9: KOL/NKOL, Engage the product metrics with customer profile table to drive these values, if a customer has a flag 1, then it would be Key opinion leader, otherwise not
KPIs

Columns: KPIs, Business Definition, Calculation
Row 1: # Interactions (2-Way), # of Unique HCP Interactions = #Interactions KOL + #Interactions Non-KOL

# of Unique HCA Interactions = #Interactions Non-KOL

All Activity corresponding to following Record Type Names:
Event_vod, Interactions, Medical Non-Verified
MSL Meeting Brief_vod, Count(Distinct Call ID) where Record Type Name in ("Event_vod",
"Interactions","Medical Non Verified",
"MSL Meeting Brief_vod")
Row 2: # Interactions KOL, No of Interactions to KOL Accounts, Count(Distinct Call ID) where KOL Flag=1 and Record Type Name in ("Event_vod", 
"Interactions", "Medical Non Verified", 
"MSL Meeting Brief_vod")
Row 3: # Interactions Non-KOL, No of Interactions to Non-KOL Accounts, Count(Distinct Call ID) where KOL Flag=0 and Record Type Name in ("Event_vod",
"Interactions",
"Medical Non Verified",
"MSL Meeting Brief_vod")
Row 4: # Interaction prod Touchpoints, Total number of people the MSL had a 2-way exchange with X the total products/disease states discussed, (#Interaction Prod Touchpoints KOL + #Interaction Prod Touchpoints Non-KOL) x products/disease states discussed
Row 5: # Interaction Touchpoints KOL, All unique Interaction engagements corresponding to KOL accounts and products, # Interactions with KOL x # Prod Discussed
Row 6: # Interaction Touchpoints Non-KOL, All unique Interaction engagements corresponding to Non-KOL accounts and products, # Interactions with Non-KOL x # Prod Discussed
Row 7: # Presentation (1-Way), Unique Presentation Meetings (i.e. Only parent meeting)
All Activity corresponding to:
Record Type Name: "Presentation", Count(Distinct Parent Call ID) where KOL Flag=1 and Record Type Name="Presentation"
Row 8: # Presentation Attendees, Number of attendees entered in Veeva Medical (number value), Sum(# Presentation Attendees)
Row 9: # Presentation Attendees KOL, #Presentation Attendee as KOL, Count(Distinct Call ID) where Record Type Name="Presentation" and KOL Flag=1
Row 10: # Presentation Attendees Non-KOL, #Presentation Attendee as Non-KOL, Count(Distinct Call ID) where Record Type Name="Presentation" and KOL Flag=0
Row 11: # Presentation Attendees Others, # of Unique Presentation Instances corresponding to Non-Profiled attendees, (# Presentation Attendees) - (# Presentation Attendees KOL + # Presentation Attendees Non-KOL)

#Presentation Attendee (Non-Profiled)
Row 12: # Presentation Prod Touchpoints, Total number of people the MSL had a 1-way exchange with X the total products/disease states discussed, Total Number of Presentation Attendees entered in Veeva Medical (number value) x # Prod Discussed
Row 13: # Presentation Touchpoints KOL, All unique Presentation engagements corresponding to KOL accounts and products, # Presentation with KOL x # Prod Discussed
Row 14: # Presentation Touchpoints Non-KOL, All unique Presentation engagements corresponding to Non-KOL accounts and products, # Presentation with Non-KOL x # Prod Discussed
Row 15: # Presentation Touchpoints Others, All unique Presentation engagements corresponding to Non-profiled accounts and products, Total # of Presentations Other (2d above) x # Prod Discussed
Row 16: # Total Interact/Present Attendees, Total number of people with whom the MSL interacted, (# Interactions) + (# Presentation Attendees)
Row 17: # Total Interact/Present Attendees KOL, (Total Number of Presentation Attendees entered in Veeva Medical (number value)), #Interactions Attendees + #Presentation Attendees
Row 18: # Total Interact/Present Attendees Non-KOL, # of Unique Interaction or Presentation Instances corresponding to Non-KOL accounts, (# Interactions Non-KOL) + (# Presentation Attendees Non-KOL)
Row 19: # Total Interact/Present Attendees Others, Calculates only presentation attendees as all interactions are against profiled attendees, #Presentation Attendee as Others (Non Profiled)
Row 20: # Product Touchpoints, Total number of people the MSL interacted with x the total products/disease states discussed, #Prod Touchpoints KOL + #Prod Touchpoints Non-KOL + #Prod Touchpoints Others
Row 21: # Product Touchpoints KOL, All unique engagements corresponding to KOL accounts and products, Total Interact/Present KOL (#Interaction Prod Touchpoints KOL + #Presentation Prod Touchpoints KOL) x # Prod Discussed
Row 22: # Product Touchpoints Non-KOL, All unique engagements corresponding to Non-KOL accounts and products, Total Interact/Present Non- KOL (#Interaction Prod Touchpoints Non-KOL + #Presentation Prod Touchpoints Non- KOL) x # Prod Discussed
Row 23: # Product Touchpoints Others, All unique engagements corresponding to Non-profiled accounts and products, Calculates only presentation attendees as all interactions are against profiled attendees. Total # of Presentations Other (2d above) x # Prod Discussed
Row 24: Unique KOL Reached, No of KOL physicians who have been called, Count(Distinct CCT ID) where (KOL Flag=1 and Call ID<>NULL)
Row 25: Unique N-KOL Reached, No of N-KOL physicians who have been called, Count(Distinct CCT ID) where (KOL Flag=0 and Call ID<>NULL)
Row 26: # MIR Requests, No of medical Inquiries, Count(Distinct MIR ID) where status in Closed MSL, Closed MIS, Saved and Open
Row 27: HCP Interactions by Month, # Calls to physicians by Month, Count(Distinct Call ID) per Quarter
Row 28: % Interactions Aligned to Strategy, % Calls by Strategic Imperative, Count (Distinct Call ID) per Strategy / Total Count(Distinct Call ID)
Row 29: # Closed MIR Requests, No of medical Inquiries that are closed/resolved, Count(Distinct MIR ID) where status in Closed MSL, Closed MIS
Row 30: # Saved MIR Requests, No of medical Inquiries that are saved, Count(Distinct MIR ID) where status in Saved
Row 31: # Open MIR Requests, No of medical Inquiries that are open and are yet to be resolved, Count(Distinct MIR ID) where status in Open
Row 32: Interactions by Product, # Calls by Product, Count (Distinct Call ID) per Product / Total Count(Distinct Call ID)
Row 33: Interactions by Activity Type, # Calls by Activity type, Count (Distinct Call ID) per Activity Type/ Total Count(Distinct Call ID)
Row 34: Interactions by Channel, # Calls by Channels, Count (Distinct Call ID) per Channel/ Total Count(Distinct Call ID)
Row 35: Medical Requests by Channel, # MIRs by Channel, Count(Distinct MIR ID) per Channel
Row 36: Medical Requests by Product, # MIRs by Product, Count(Distinct MIR ID) per Product
Row 37: MIR Trend by Quarter, # MIRs by Quarters, by Status, Count(Distinct MIR ID) per Quarter
Row 38: # Responses, Number of responses to a Survey Question, Count({<[Statement Value Description]-={''}>}[CCT ID])
Row 39: % Response, % of a Responses for each survey question, Count({<[Statement Value Description]-={''}>}[CCT ID])/count({<[Statement Value Description]-={''}>}total <[Survey Name],Question>[Statement Value Description])

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Appendix
Project Summary
Measurement and reporting aspects of the Medical Teams. The focus of this project is to finalize the requirements which includes defining and designing the KPIs.
The Approach includes:
Conducting Requirements Workshop
Understand the Veeva ecosystem for reporting objectives
section_path: Understand the Veeva ecosystem for reporting objectives

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Finalize KPIs and metrics for capturing the details corresponding to interactions, presentations, MIRs, Mindsets and other significant metrics
section_path: Understand the Veeva ecosystem for reporting objectives

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Align of the data sources and data files, required for reporting
section_path: Align of the data sources and data files, required for reporting

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Review and align on business rules
section_path: Review and align on business rules

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Dashboard design screen mockups
Data Gathering and Integration
Align of the data sources and data files, required for reporting
section_path: Align of the data sources and data files, required for reporting

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Review business rules definitions
Dashboard Design and Development
Review dashboard screen design and mockups
section_path: Review dashboard screen design and mockups

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Develop, test and deploy dashboards
Purpose of the Document

This document captures detailed user requirements to build a tool to enhance Business Analytics capabilities of Sanofi team to analyze the Medical Reporting Ecosystem, by leveraging the centrally developed and supported CIM system and building an Ad-hoc analytics layer on top of it. This new Ad-hoc Analytics layer would be based on Qlik Sense platform and would be primarily developed for Sanofi team to carry out different analysis including field leadership, Regional Medical Liaisons, HQ staff and senior executives.
Architecture Model QS

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Sanofi’s PSS A&R team is looking to evolve its analytics ecosystem to align with Digital’s OneMesh / Data Mesh principles, enabling scalable, reusable, and AI-ready data assets.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.source_file: PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowflake.docx
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowflake.docx
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: The objective of this engagement is to design and develop Consumption-Aligned Data Assets (CADs) and Reporting Ready Datasets (RRDs). These data assets will be aligned with Digital’s OneMesh data principles and are AI-ready for future “Talk to Data” capabilities to enable OnePSS dashboards with built-in KPIs, metadata, and configurable business rules. We will be leveraging existing Process-Aligned Data Assets (PADs) owned and managed by Sanofi ICI team

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text:
Columns: Key Activities, Details, Expected Outcomes/Deliverables
Row 1: Onboarding and Requirements Gathering, Conduct formal kickoff sessions with Sanofi stakeholders to align objectives, scope, governance, and ways of working
Establish communication plan, governance cadence (steering, working sessions), and initial project plan
Provision access for the project team to required systems and tools (e.g., Snowflake, Informatica, Confluence, SharePoint)
Finalize team structure, RACI, and onboarding plan for all stakeholders
Structured onboarding to Sanofi ecosystem and review of existing ICI PAD data models, architecture, DQ framework and design rationale
Gather requirements into a business requirement document template and validate business requirements from PSS A&R stakeholders, Onboarding: domain access, infrastructure and other tools and tech accesses set up for each team member.
Established program governance cadence and operating model
Duly filled and approved business requirements document for PSS A&R use cases (Dupixent)
JIRA user stories
Row 2: Requirements Mapping, Map approved PSS A&R business requirements to available source systems and existing ICI-owned PADs, including identification of data grain and KPI calculation logic.
Validation and sign-off of Dupixent PSS A&R mappings by Sanofi stakeholders prior to design initiation., Requirements-to-data mapping document (Dupixent, Other TAs and Adhoc use cases)
Signed-off mapping documentation
Row 3: Design, Work with Sanofi Architects/ICI team on designing requirements to Data Assets mapping
Finalization and preparation of DQ checks
Preparation of High-level and low-level design for data objects including
Data Models
Source to Target Mapping
Metadata tagging, Data Assets Archetypes (Requirements to new Data Assets mapping)
Technical Design Document (Logical, Physical Data Model and STTM document)
Minimum Metadata requirements and metadata template
Finalized DQ checklist
Row 4: Build, Code as per low level design and execute unit testing
Metadata addition
Addition of DQ checks
End to end automation and orchestration configurations, Data Pipelines and code repositories
Unit Test documents 
E2E Code review with Sanofi
Row 5: SIT, Creation of Test Strategy, Test scripts
Preparation / Execution of test cases considering positive and negative data scenarios
End to end testing of all modules
Document SIT results, defects, and co-ordinate with development team for defect resolution.
Orchestration testing with dry run of end-to-end automated jobs within the application, Test Strategy
Test Case scripts 
Test execution report (E2E)
Row 6: UAT, UAT environment setup and data loads
Prepare UAT Test Cases/Scripts.
Collaborate with Sanofi BOS users and support them with executing & performing UAT validations. 
Defect Management – Defect Triaging and resolution
Confirm both technical and user readiness for go-live.
Define DevOps Governance and Operating Model, UAT Test Plan document
UAT case scripts preparation
UAT test execution report and result
Paybook for Operational process and DevOps governance
Row 7: Hypercare and Downstream Retrofitment, Perform hyper-care support activities, including monitor processes, provide support if needed, bug resolution and triage.
Creation of required cutover and knowledge transfer documentation and KT workshops
Provide documentation, oversight, SOPs, and advisory support for 6 weeks to enable downstream Power BI dashboard retrofitment by Sanofi PSS A&R HUB teams, Hypercare support and handover artifacts (SOP documents)
Operations, enhancement, and KPI governance documentation

Row 1: Requirements Gathering and Onboarding, Draft a business requirement documents template
Update the requirements for Dupixent PSS A&R reporting needs
Review of existing ICI PAD data models, architecture, DQ framework and design

Deliverables – Completed and Approved Business requirement document for PSS A&R use cases (Dupixent), Update Business requirements document template with other ICI downstream use cases
Onboarding of PSS A&R team on ICI data models and DQ framework





Deliverables – Completed and Approved Business requirement document for Downstream use cases, Fill Business Requirements Document template for PSS A&R Other TAs & ad-hoc use-cases
Review and validate the requirements for Dupixent pre-filled by ZS team





Deliverables – Completed and Approved BRD for Other TAs and Ad-hoc requirements
Row 2: Mapping Requirements, Map approved PSS A&R business requirements to available source systems and existing ICI-owned PADs






Deliverables – Signed off Dupixent, Other TAs and Ad-hoc use cases Requirements to Data asset mapping, Map gathered Downstream user requirements to their existing PADs and source data







Deliverables – Signed off other ICI Downstream Requirements to Data asset mapping, Provide business inputs, validate & signoff on the data mappings (drafted by ZS)
Provide business inputs, review, validate & signoff on the Dupixent, Other TAs, ad-hoc use-cases and data mappings (drafted by ZS, Sanofi ICI)
Row 3: Design, Co-design and finalize the target CADs data architecture
Draft High-level, logical, physical data model and STTM documentation, Metadata and DQ checks for 6 data assets (Assuming 1 CAD and 5 RRDs)


Deliverables – Requirements to Data asset Archetypes (co-led), 
Logical and Physical Data Model, Source to Target Mapping documentation for ZS owned Data asset. Metadata layout documentation. Finalized DQ checks, Co-design and finalize the target CADs data architecture
Draft High-level, logical, physical data model and STTM, Metadata and DQ checks for 6 data assets (Assuming 1 CAD and 5 RRDs)


Deliverables – Requirements to Data asset Archetypes (co-led), Logical and Physical Data Model, Source to Target Mapping documentation for Sanofi ICI owned Data Assets
Metadata layout documentation. Finalized DQ checks, Review, validate & signoff on the Data Architecture, Logical & Physical Data Models, Source to Target Mapping documentation (drafted by ZS, Sanofi ICI)
Row 4: Build, Development and Unit Testing of 6 data assets (assuming 1 CAD and 5 RRDs)
Metadata addition
DQ checks addition
Alignment on deployment plan and ownership

Deliverables – Complete build & Unit testing for ZS owned Data Assets. Metadata and DQ Addition for all Data Assets, Development and Unit Testing 6 data assets (assuming 1 CAD and 5 RRDs)
Alignment on deployment plan and ownership

Deliverables – Complete build & Unit testing for Sanofi ICI owned Data Assets.
Metadata and DQ Addition for all Data Assets, Code Review, validate & signoff on the Implementation of Data Assets, Metadata and DQs (owned by ZS, Sanofi ICI)
Row 5: SIT, Creation of test strategy and test scripts for ZS owned CAD and RRDs
Defect fixing, Triaging and End to End testing of all ZS owned tables


Deliverables – Test strategy, cases, scripts and Test execution report for ZS owned Data Assets, Creation of test strategy and test scripts for Sanofi ICI owned CADs and RRDs
Defect fixing, Triaging and End to End testing of all Sanofi ICI owned tables

Deliverables – Test strategy, cases, scripts and Test execution report for Sanofi ICI owned Data Assets, SIT Test Case Review, validate & signoff on the SIT execution of Data Assets, Metadata and DQs (owned by ZS, Sanofi ICI)
Row 6: UAT, UAT Test cases and test scripts draft for 6 data assets (assuming 1 CAD and 5 RRDs)
Participate and support in the UAT execution with sanofi A&R teams, Feedback incorporation and updates to the ZS owned tables
Co-designing playbook (including PSS A&R specific use cases and scenarios) for DevOps Governance and Operating Model


Deliverables – Test strategy, cases, scripts and UAT execution report for ZS owned Data Assets. Co-designed Operations and Governance Playbooks, UAT Test cases and test scripts draft for 6 data assets (assuming 1 CAD and 5 RRDs)
UAT execution, Feedback incorporation and updates to the Sanofi ICI owned tables
Co-designing playbook for DevOps Governance and Operating Model
Obtaining signoff on downstream requirements UAT

Deliverables – Test strategy, cases, scripts and UAT execution report for Sanofi ICI owned Data Assets. Co-designed Operations and Governance Playbooks, Participate in the UAT for both ZS developed and Sanofi ICI developed CADs and RRDs
UAT signoff post successful execution on PSS A&R requirements
Row 7: Hypercare and Downstream Retrofitment, Post go-live hypercare support (documentation, KT workshops, defect fixing) for 6 data assets (assuming 1 CAD and 5 RRDs)
Oversight support of 6 weeks for providing guidance on dashboard Retrofitment

Deliverables – Hypercare handover artifacts (SOPs, KT documents) and Retrofitment SOPs, Post go-live operations support (documentation & defect fixing) for 6 data assets (assuming 
1 CAD and 5 RRDs), Retrofitment of PSS A&R Power BI dashboards












Deliverables – Retrofitted Power BI Dashboards

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: The scope of this project is to enhance the existing OneCI setup with the following activities in-scope:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text:
Columns: Category, Activities
Row 1: Medical, Conduct requirements gathering sessions(1-2) with LUCI and OneCI Team
Design 2 new medical views, along with adding their source views to the OneCRM Foundation Data Mart
Update the current IICS mappings across 3 layers - staging, DWH and Data Mart for OneCRM
Create URS/FRS and STTM, Data models document to incorporate OneCI above country medical changes.
Updates in the existing approved URS/FRS documents for OneCRM and OneCI
Co-ordination with OneCI Team, LUCI Team, One CRM
Row 2: Data Quality checks, Design & build for business data quality checks, according to the requirements provided by LUCI
URS/FRS updates to reflect the DQ requirements, TDD updates
Creation of DQ queries for required scenarios, integration of DQ module
End to end testing and sign-off

Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Row 1: Onshore ZS Team, Onshore ZS Team
Row 2: Senior Business Analyst/Integration Lead, Has good domain knowledge around Sanofi data and business applications.
Engage and communicate with Sanofi stakeholders as required during product requirements definition, design, and development.
Coordination with onshore team on data findings and data analysis results
Row 3: Offshore ZS Team, Offshore ZS Team
Row 4: Technical Team Lead, Responsible for day-to-day management of project
Responsible to maintaining timelines and quality of all deliverables.
Provide oversight of technical solution design and implementation
Ensure adherence to Sanofi program management framework and standards in delivery.
Row 5: Sr. Developer, Review requirements and design and raise clarifying questions before starting build.
Review design and develop POCs along with the design team.
Review and contribute to the design with the overall solution in mind.
Lead and perform code reviews, with the understanding of overall solution and client coding standards.
Lead with the Design/build activities.
Manage day to day build activities and coordinate with offshore delivery lead to ensure progress is being made as per plan.
Row 6: Test Analyst, Prepare test cases based on scenarios defined
For an issues / concerns around design, requirement and scenarios – raise the same in defect tracker and discuss the same on daily calls 
Create test status report tool and share daily status report using the tool
During UAT phase, retest UAT defects in SIT before they are delivered to UAT for retest

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text:
Columns: Area, Risks, Mitigation Approach
Row 1: Requirements, Managing unknown
requirements / processes, ZS will closely coordinate with OneCI team to help in proactively pre-empting any issues related to missed requirements and any unknown requirements uncovered after requirements phase and that impacts project timelines will be assessed through Change Control
Row 2: Change Requests, Changes to scope or delivery schedule, delay project delivery and increase project management costs., Assign a single point of contact from Sanofi Digital as the product owner who is accountable for overall project plan and liaisons with other Sanofi functions.
Weekly status updates meetings with the core team to highlight potential issues early to minimize impact on timeline. Periodic Steering Committee meetings to highlight / escalate issues.
Any changes to scope will be assessed through Change Control
Row 3: Delivery, Unavailability of downstream teams for testing, Both ZS and downstream teams to get aligned on the timeline and agree on the complete set of integration test scenarios during requirements phase, any deviations from the agreed upon timeline to be managed through Change Control
Row 4: OneCI/LUCI team’s availability, Timely onboarding of managed services partner and their availability, Sanofi Digital to plan production operations team in a time bound manner

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Project Summary

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: The scope of Project Diamond is to transforms the on-premise data warehouses DSS (Decision Support System) and SGDW (Sanofi Genzyme Datawarehouse) and build a common Cloud based Datawarehouse platform for its Genzyme Specialty and General Medicine divisions to support U.S commercial business applications. Through this initiative, Sanofi will develop an enterprise data management platform that will improve the advanced analytics capabilities and will prove to be a competitive advantage for Sanofi over its competitors.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: DSS & SGDW currently run on on-prem Informatica Power Center and Oracle and have grown increasingly complex and the evolution in the business needs is nearly continuous. Building the next generation data warehouse application on the cloud creates enormous opportunity to define a new model for innovation, operation and transformation to get ahead of the pace of the business.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: As part of this initiative, ZS will design, build and deploy all processes, that exist in current DSS and SGDW platforms, to the new cloud-based data warehouse platform based on AWS / Informatica Cloud (IICS) / Snowflake.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: In order to gain quicker benefits of the new platform and mitigate program execution risk, this project approaches the onboarding of business units in in an agile manner through multiple releases and ZS will follow Sanofi’s Agile delivery methodology using POD structure and use tools like JIRA and Confluence.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Scalability: Build configurable frameworks and loosely coupled modules that allow faster onboarding of new datasets and scalable design for new enhancements. The design will consider future re-usability and scalability of common components to other countries
section_path: Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Optimization of data process: Opportunity to improve current data process and underlying business process as identified by business stakeholders in requirements and design session aligned to the global data governance framework.
section_path: Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Accelerated Performance: Accelerate processes within the system through point to point integration across applications and effective utilization of the cloud infrastructure to process sales data faster by 25-30% or more
section_path: Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Data Quality: Configurable data quality engine with business DQ checks for errors that commonly affect downstream business applications that will significantly improve quality of data across the platform and delivered to downstream business applications
section_path: Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Eliminate limitations due to data volume: Current legacy platform limits ability to ingest complex and large volume datasets like APLD etc. These limitations will be eliminated by moving to cloud and optimizing / redesigning current data integration processes
section_path: Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: As aligned with Sanofi enterprise architecture for Cloud Platforms, below layers will be created for Cloud based DSS / SGDW as part of Project Diamond:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Data Collection Ingestion

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: All Data sources are expected to provide data feeds for each release in a common SFTP layer or defined AWS S3 buckets or via Snowflake data sharing mechanism for Project Diamond consumption

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: For Data Initiation into the new platform, data sources that provide incremental / delta files are expected to provide one-time history of the data to be initiated / processed. In case the data provider is unable to send the full feed, assuming that Quantum has the history of all the delta files sent from the source, ZS will work with the Snowflake team to migrate Historical data from Quantum. Any data transformations required to migrate Historical data will be evaluated via the change request process

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Data sources that provide full history in every feed are expected to provide full files (as in BAU process) on the common SFTP layer / AWS S3 bucket

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Below is the major scope of the change management activities:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: S. No, User Group, Change Management Activities
Row 1: 1, Downstream Commercial Operations Business Applications, Downstream commercial applications like IC, FD, FR, BI, Veeva are major consumers of data from DSS / SGDW. Below are the high-level change management activities for these applications:
Data comparison and Training materials:
Perform data comparison between new and legacy datasets and highlight the discrepancies, if any
Prepare training materials to educate the downstream applications to start consuming data from the new cloud platform
Cut-over Strategy: 
As downstream business applications are dependent on Frozen Quarterly process, change management and a cut-over strategy will be planned to align with the frozen timelines and to minimize the impact 
Communication Plan
Establish the communication plan for downstream applications for awareness regarding system cut-off from legacy to new system
Discuss and plan the parallel run, cutover to new system.
Row 2: 2, Upstream Data Sources / Vendor Teams, Upstream sources integrate with DSS / SGDW for feeding data such as facts and dimensions. The major impact on such systems will be on the interface of integration. 
Interface Changes
Work with the respective upstream application IT teams to coordinate the change in delivery mechanism and path
Data Sources may have to feed to both legacy DSS / SGDW and Project Diamond for certain period and that will be planned and coordinated with the respective data vendor teams

Any delay due to environment or product related issues may impact the cost and timelines

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Sanofi Digital / Data Management will provide sign-offs on the requirements, design and other testing deliverables in a timely fashion; delays in obtaining sign-offs will directly affect the delivery timelines and cost.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Existing AMS operations teams for DSS and SGDW will be available during Project Diamond implementation to clarify any questions on the processes or data in existing platforms

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx

For processes where there is no documentation available, ZS will collaborate with Sanofi to document the process during requirements phase

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Currently, all inbound data sources into DSS and outbound data sources to platforms like Veeva, RCM are sent via Integration Hub. As Integration Hub is not part of the architecture for Project Diamond, it is assumed that all exchanges that were done via Integration Hub will be done via SFTP or AWS S3. Any data transformations currently done in Integration Hub prior to sending data to DSS are assumed to be managed by the new replacement platform for Integration Hub. If any of these assumptions change, the impact will be analyzed first and corresponding changes will be managed via change management process.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Project Diamond will plan to integrate with the new US Commercial Product MDM. For timely integration, the overall timelines of new US Product MDM should sync up with Project Diamond releases and should be confirmed during Project Diamond Requirements/Design Phase. It is assumed that data models / layouts and interfacing mechanism for new US Product MDM into Project Diamond will be provided during Project Diamond Requirements/Design phase. Any challenges with the alignment of new US Product MDM timelines with Project Diamond timelines will impact the overall Project Diamond schedule and will be assessed via change control process.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: In case of delay with the new US Product MDM or the timelines do not sync up with Project Diamond, Project Diamond will interface with existing DSS based Product master.  A decision of the same will be collectively made between Sanofi Digital and ZS and no later than the Design phase. In this case, Project Diamond will be designed to be scalable and flexible to simplify integration with new US Product MDM at a later stage which will be done through change control.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: The scope of the SOW includes migration of DSS and SGDW to Cloud. All applicable modules such as Business Rules Engine (BRE), Data Quality Manager (DQM) and Orchestration, will be set-up common for both DSS and SGDW

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: In case of establishing Snowflake to Snowflake data exchange between IQVIA and Project Diamond, it is assumed that IQVIA will apply all applicable existing rules/transformations/data filters prior to publishing the data to Sanofi.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Project Management, Requirements gathering and Change Management for both DSS & SGDW to be aligned to achieve cost and resource synergies

ZS will collaborate with Sanofi to determine if a change request is required to reapply these changes in the cloud environment. Any minor enhancements such as bug fixes, minor critical business requirements (less than 80 hours), up to a reasonable limit and without extending the project timeline, will be covered as part of the project. Any major changes such as onboarding a new data source or addition of a new BU etc. will go through change control process

Activities that need design, code or testing changes that are not included as part of current scope, will be considered as enhancements. If the requested changes/enhancements are beyond the original scope of the project, and/or exceed the overall timeline of the project, a separate change request will be raised with additional cost to extend the project scope/timeline

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: In order to speed up the delivery of work products, to cause minimal interruption to downstream applications and to achieve a flexible delivery model, this project follows agile approach for implementation.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: The requirements will be identified and defined during project initiation as part of the Foundation phase and post the Foundation phase, each release will be implemented through multiple sprints.

A tentative priority order of onboarding BUs is mentioned below, which will be reviewed with Sanofi and revised if needed within the overall timeline of the project.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Foundation: This phase includes collecting overall requirements, defining the architecture and setting up the cloud foundation in AWS / Snowflake / Informatica Cloud based on best practice guidance from Sanofi

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Roles & Responsibilities

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: ZS understands that the success of this project is driven by right mix of people with technology and domain expertise. ZS is committed to deploy a technical team well-versed in the technologies such as AWS, Snowflake and Informatica to ensure success for Project Diamond.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: If at any time, the services provided by a ZS resource is not in accordance with Sanofi’s expectations, ZS will have maximum of 2 weeks to correct the matter. In the even it is not resolved, Sanofi has the right to request a replacement and ZS will take immediate action. ZS will notify Sanofi of any lead time required to replace the resource for full transparency.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: To ensure that Project Diamond design and architecture are being deployed as per best practices,  ZS will engage with its AWS, Snowflake and Informatica partners as necessary and at various points so that Sanofi can get maximum benefits out of the technical tools.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Following is a description of high-level roles and responsibilities of key ZS and Sanofi Resources:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: ZS roles & responsibilities:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Row 1: Onshore ZS Team, Onshore ZS Team
Row 2: Program Sponsor, Strategic advisor to the project team
Ensures the highest quality client service across all projects and work streams
Row 3: Engagement Manager, Overall engagement relationships accountability
Responsible for overall delivery and client satisfaction
Ensures that expertise is always available to the client by engaging the right ZS experts for each project task
Row 4: Solution / Technical Architect, Expertise in AWS/ Informatica Cloud/ Snowflake and Lead by implementing best practices in these technologies
Review the ongoing implementation solution for improvement
opportunities
Lead feasibility analysis, select the technologies that provide the
best solution and identify the products available that will best fit
the solution proposed
Row 5: Senior Business Analyst/Integration Lead, Has good domain knowledge around Sanofi data and business applications
Engage and communicate with Sanofi stakeholders as required during product requirements definition, design and development 
Coordination with onshore team on data findings and data analysis results
Row 6: Project/Testing coordinator, Accountability for communication and management of status, risks, and issues
Conduct status meetings; manage product backlog and track progress
Row 7: Offshore ZS Team, Offshore ZS Team
Row 8: Offshore Sanofi Delivery Lead, Accountable for the delivery of the all the Sanofi projects from India
Ensures that all the Sanofi workstreams are well connected and share learnings
Row 9: Technical Team Lead, Responsible for day-to-day management of project
Responsible to maintaining timelines and quality of all deliverables
Provide oversight of technical solution design and implementation
Ensure adherence to Sanofi program management framework and standards in delivery
Row 10: Sr. Developer, Review requirements and design and raise clarifying questions before starting build
Review design and develop POCs along with the design team
Review and contribute to the design with the overall solution in mind
Lead and perform code reviews, with the understanding of overall solution and client coding standards
Lead with the Design/build activities 
Manage day to day build activities and coordinate with offshore delivery lead to ensure progress is being made as per plan
Row 11: Developer(s), Work on building application based on the approved requirements and design
Reconfigure Business Rules which are required 
Establish Data Model /design and development guidelines and best practices 
Develop code and perform unit testing
Row 12: Solution/Technical Architect, Provide guidance to the team in designing the solution taking advantage of the existing assets maintaining a balance between architecture requirements and specific client need
Expertise in AWS/ Informatica Cloud/ Snowflake and Lead by implementing best practices in these technologies
Row 13: Test Lead, Create test scenarios for system integration testing and map them to requirements in RTM 
Conducts periodic reviews of test cases prepared by associates to ensure positive and negative scenarios are covered and track progress 
Ensures UAT defects are tested first in SIT before they are delivered to UAT for retest
Conduct RCA for cancelled and missed defects and implement corrective actions as per discussion with test lead /manager
Row 14: Test Analyst (s), Prepare test cases based on scenarios defined
For an issues / concerns around design, requirement and scenarios – raise the same in defect tracker and discuss the same on daily calls 
Create test status report tool and share daily status report using the tool
During UAT phase, retest UAT defects in SIT before they are delivered to UAT for retest
Row 15: AWS, Snowflake, IICS Professional Services, AWS, Snowflake, IICS Professional Services
Row 16: Snowflake Solutions Architect, Detailed architecture review and support during Design as per Snowflake best practices
Advise on design best practices for integrating with Informatica Cloud
Advise on Integration with interfacing Snowflake based platforms like IQVIA, Reltio 
Priority support to debug any show stopper issues in Snowflake
Resolve any performance issues during test cycles
Support for Historical data migration from legacy platform for incremental data sources (where source does not provide full history)
Immediate priority support for Snowflake issues during production runs
Row 17: Informatica Solutions Architect, Detailed architecture review and support during Design as per best practices for IICS and integration with Snowflake
Guidance and review for IICS design and share best practices to make scalable process
Row 18: Informatica Solutions Delivery Consultant, Priority support to debug any show stopper issues related to IICS or IICS-Snowflake integration
Resolve any performance issues during test cycles
Support any code migration from Informatica on-prem to IICS (if required)
Priority support for IICS issues during production runs
Row 19: AWS Solutions Architect, Detailed architecture review and support during Design as per AWS best practices
Advise on design best practices for hosting Snowflake and Informatica platforms

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Following is a description of roles and responsibilities from Sanofi’s side which would be working in close co-ordination with ZS.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Row 1: Program Manager, Act at the liaison between ZS and Sanofi leadership
Row 2: PMO, Work in tandem with ZS Project/Testing Coordinator to collaborate with various Sanofi core team/downstream/Infrastructure teams
Provide status updates to Sanofi core team
Row 3: System Owner, Overall business requirement/demand owner
Help with DSS / SGDW retirement plan from a Sanofi standpoint
Row 4: User Project Leader (UPL), Define & Approve user requirements
Provide user requirements for processes where documentation is not available
Provide inputs to come up with effective system retirement and data migration plan
Row 5: Technical Architect, Review & Approve design documents and architecture
Row 6: IS Project Leader (IPL), Review & Approve design documents and architecture
Approve code migration and deployments
Review & Approve test strategy/test cases/test results
Row 7: Validation Expert (VEX), Help define validation plan and PUMA deliverables for the project
Provide inputs to define test strategy, retirement plan and data migration plan
Row 8: Business Quality Representative, Provide business inputs to formulate test strategy

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Risk Management

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Detailed RAID (Risk, Action, Issue, Decision) log process establishment during the project planning and initiation phase will help in early identification of risks and issues and thus taking timely actions and decisions accordingly.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Following are some key risks and their mitigation approach:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Area, Risks, Mitigation Approach
Row 1: Requirements, Managing unknown
requirements / processes, ZS will closely coordinate with Sanofi Data Management team to help in proactively pre-empting any issues related to missed requirements and any unknown requirements uncovered after requirements phase and that impacts project timelines will be assessed through Change Control
Row 2: Onboarding, Delay in Onboarding of ZS resources to Sanofi network and Project Diamond tool stack, Sanofi Digital to help initiate onboarding of ZS resources prior to initiation of the project to avoid any delays to Project Timelines
Row 3: Change Requests, Changes to scope or delivery schedule, delay project delivery and increase project management costs, Assign a single point of contact from Sanofi Digital as the product owner who is accountable for overall project plan and liaisons with other Sanofi functions
Weekly status updates meetings with the core team to highlight potential issues early to minimize impact on timeline. Periodic Steering Committee meetings to highlight / escalate issues
Any changes to scope will be assessed through Change Control
Row 4: Infrastructure, Unavailability of Sanofi GIS for code migration across environments or resolving infrastructure issues, Sanofi Digital /GIS will identify appropriate backup resources to resolve infrastructure issues as well as expedite code/data migration requests
Row 5: Delivery, Unavailability of downstream teams for testing, Both ZS and downstream teams to get aligned on the timeline and agree on the complete set of integration test scenarios during requirements phase, any deviations from the agreed upon timeline to be managed through Change Control
Row 6: Transition, Timely onboarding of managed services partner and their availability, Sanofi Digital to plan production operations team in a time bound manner and onboard them prior to the first release of BU (Immunology) into Project Diamond
Row 7: Product MDM, Syncing new US Commercial Product MDM design, testing, go-live timelines with Project Diamond, Finalize timelines for syncing each release of Project Diamond with Product MDM during requirements phase. Close co-ordination is required between Project Diamond and US Product MDM team at the start of every release to re-assess the syncing of testing, go-live timelines. Any changes required due to the delay in planned timelines will be assessed via change control

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: The project plan and pricing set forth in this Sanofi project diamond DSS & SGDW Migration do not take into consideration any price escalations, delays, shortages, and other disruptions that may occur due to the global COVID-19 (also known as coronavirus) pandemic and actions taken in response to the pandemic by businesses, local, state, and national authorities, or by ZS in its reasonable discretion (collectively, a “COVID-19 Event). Sanofi acknowledges and agrees that ZS is permitted to adjust the timelines, fees and/or personnel assignments set forth in this SOW if, in its reasonable judgement, ZS determines that a Covid-19 Event will prevent it from delivering the project as set forth in this SOW. ZS will provide Sanofi with prompt written notice of any such determination and, upon Sanofi’s request, will execute a change order to this SOW documenting any such changes.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Term, Description
Row 1: API, Application Program Interface
Row 2: AWS, Amazon Web services
Row 3: BAU, Business as usual
Row 4: BIA, Business Intelligence & Analytics; refers to DW&BI applications in Sanofi ecosystem
Row 5: BRE, Business Rule Engine
Row 6: BU, Business Unit
Row 7: CARS, Contract Administration and Reporting System
Row 8: CIM, Customer Information Management; refers to the system with SAS datasets that provide analytics to downstream commercial operation applications
Row 9: DDD, Drug Distribution Data. Sales data of direct and indirect sales at the zip code level (retail) or at the outlet level (non-retail/hospital) every month.
Row 10: DQF, Data Quality Framework
Row 11: DSS, Decision Support System. The module of Quantum that deals with sales and marketing information and the analysis of that information to assist in creating forecasts.
Row 12: ETL, Extract, Transform, Load
Row 13: DW, Data Warehouse
Row 14: FO, Field Operations
Row 15: ITS, IT Services
Row 16: IICS, Informatica cloud services
Row 17: IMM, Immunology
Row 18: IQVIA, Provider of Customer & Sales data
Row 19: Integration Hub, The central component connecting DSS and the various customer data consumers. It also stages the raw files from the various source systems.
Row 20: Liquid Hub, Specialty Pharmacy Data Aggregator and Source Provider
Row 21: MS, Multiple Sclerosis
Row 22: ONC, Oncology
Row 23: PDB, Patient Database
Row 24: PMO, Project Management Office
Row 25: RBD, Rare blood disorder
Row 26: RCM, Regional Customer Master
Row 27: RD, Rare disease
Row 28: S3, Simple storage services
Row 29: SAS, Statistical Analysis System is a software suite developed by the SAS Institute for advanced analytics, multivariate analyses, business intelligence, data management, and predictive analytics.
Row 30: SFTP, Stands for “Secure File Transfer Protocol” , is a network protocol that provides file transfer and manipulation functionality over any reliable data stream
Row 31: SGDW, Sanofi Genzyme Data Warehouse
Row 32: TRACE, Refers to the system/application that handles promotional activities/events for Sanofi employees and CSO’s.
Row 33: UI, User Interface
Row 34: Veeva CRM, Veeva CRM is a platform used for customer management and helps to transform customer engagement
Row 35: VEX, Sanofi Validation Experts

The IPA is governed by the terms of the MSA dated February 22, 2021 between  and ZS Associates, Inc. (“MSA”). ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Exit Clause: With respect to the Services outlined in this SOW only, Sanofi may terminate this SOW, in whole or in part, for convenience with immediate effect. In the event this SOW is terminated for any reason, ZS shall cease performance of the Services on the date of such termination, and Sanofi shall be responsible only for payment of fees for Services performed by ZS up to termination.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Project Summary

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: As part of the ongoing project diamond releases planned for this year, there is a need from the OneCI team to update the existing OneCI Global views to include the new and missing fields and update business rules as per the documentation shared by OneCI/LUCI Team.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Additionally, there is an assumed total capacity of 90 hours: on top of the scoped list of enhancements mentioned in Table -1 under the scope section. Any additional enhancements on top of the scope will be added to the backlog, prioritized (with the product owners) and implemented within the allocated 90 hours capacity (to be planned during of sprint-3 that is inclusive of development, testing, UAT and deployment activities over a period of 3 weeks). The type of enhancements assumed in sprint-3 are minor attribute-level or business logic updates, no new data ingestion or major architectural changes are assumed to be in scope.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: The overall timeline of this scope of work is 10 weeks – From 22nd July 2024 through 27th September 2024

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: In Scope

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: The scope of this project is to enhance the existing OneCI setup with the following activities in-scope:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text:
Columns: Enhancement Subject Area, Enhancement description, Sprint
Row 1: Territory, Get territory hierarchy from Valign instead of CRM, 2
Row 2: Event, Updates in the format attribute, 1
Row 3: Interactions, Implementation of submitted interactions logic, 1
Row 4: Interactions, Integration of employee information in RTE data, 1
Row 5: Field Days, Remapping of Time of Territory values based on US CRM, 1
Row 6: Field Days, Correcting calendar and calling days logic, 1
Row 7: OCCP, Updates in the is_occp flag based on values flowing in US CRM, 1
Row 8: , Employee, same employee for all lines, 1
Row 9: Coaching, Coaching report data filtering on Survey Type to avoid duplicates, 2
Row 10: RTE, Updates in the first/last emails logics, 1
Row 11: Product, Updates in logic for disease code, 1
Row 12: CLM, Changes in CLM Interactions logics, 1
Row 13: New attributes addition, Integration of 10 new fields based on new business requirements, 2
Row 14: New attributes addition, Incorporation of 53 new timestamp attributes across 26 views, 2

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Table 1: Scope of work with known enhancement details
